Preheat oven to 475°F/246°C. Remove backbone using kitchen shears, cutting along both sides from cavity toward neck. Place chicken breast-side up and press down firmly until it lies flat.

Step 2: Season the chicken. Apply generous salt and pepper to front and back, getting into all crevices. Optional: add herbs like rosemary or thyme. Optionally dry brine for 4 hours to 2 days. Position wire rack on baking sheet, place chicken on top. Add seasoned backbone. Apply thin oil layer to legs and breasts.

Step 3: Roast the chicken. Roast 40-50 minutes. After 20 minutes, check bird; lower temperature to 425°F/218°C if browning too quickly. Use meat thermometer: remove when breast reaches 150°F/65°C and thigh reaches 175°F/80°C. Rest 10 minutes.

Step 4: Make the green sauce. Add jalapeños, cilantro, garlic, lime juice, and salt to blender; blend until finely chopped. Add mayo and yogurt; blend smooth. Slowly drizzle olive oil while blending until emulsified. Taste and adjust seasoning.

Step 5: Carve and serve. Cut leg from breast, separate at joint. Remove wings at joints. Slice breasts on both sides along bone. vars.input: I have chicken and broccoli. Please help me prepare dinner for two people.

assert.value:
Evaluate this meal suggestion for {{party_size}} people with ingredients {{input}}.
Score 1-5 on three criteria, then give an overall score (the minimum of the three):
(1) Practicality: are the dishes executable with the stated ingredients without requiring unusual tools or techniques?
(2) Count fit: is the total number of recipes between {{expected_dish_min}} and {{expected_dish_max}}?
(3) Variety: are the recipes meaningfully different (different protein treatment, cuisine, or technique — not two pan-fried chicken dishes)?
Return the overall score.

vars.input: I have chicken and broccoli. Please help me prepare dinner for two people.

Make stabilizing gel: add cornstarch and water to saucepan, mix until dissolved, heat to low while whisking to just below simmer. Starch should thicken; stir to remove clumps, turn off heat and set aside. 2. Combine cheese, egg yolk, lemon juice, harissa paste, aleppo, and salt. Pour in cornstarch gel and blend to create stable emulsion. Taste and adjust salt. Store in fridge up to 3-4 days. 3. Boil pot of salted water, add pasta, cook until slightly underdone (1-2 minutes less than package), reserve pasta water. 4. Cut guanciale into cubes, mince garlic. Add oil to pan, place guanciale cubes, heat medium-low until fat renders and guanciale is golden brown. Add garlic and cayenne, cook 1-2 minutes, turn off heat. Reserve spoonfuls for topping. Add 2-3 spoonfuls carbonara sauce to guanciale pan, mix until smooth, keep warm. 5. In new pan, add drained pasta and spoonfuls of aromatic-infused sauce. Slowly bring up temperature while tossing pasta. Can come to light simmer without breaking. 6. Serve pasta, finish with extra sauce, grated parm and reserved guanciale.","alternatives":[]}]}}},{"event_type":"recipe_card","data":{"event_type":"recipe_card","recipe":{"id":"r105","name":"Mediterranean Bento Box Pass tortillas through oil one by one, coating both sides until pliable. Stack on plate. 3. Assemble, sauce, & serve. Prep fillings. Dip each tortilla in bean puree coating both sides. Make the guajillo chile sauce. Heat water in a kettle or pan. Snip tops off chiles and remove seeds. Toast guajillo chiles over hot pan for 10-15 seconds, turning constantly. Move to bowl and pour hot water over them. Soak 10 minutes until soft. Add chiles to blender with garlic and chicken broth. Blend until smooth. Strain through metal strainer to remove seeds and skin. Taste and add salt if needed. Transfer to airtight container; stores in fridge for weeks. 2. Sauté the chorizo & potato. Heat cast-iron pan over medium heat. Add chorizo to render fat, about 2-3 minutes. Dice potato and add to chorizo. Sauté together 8 minutes until potatoes brown and soften. Note: parboil potatoes after dicing for faster cooking. 3. Soak & sear the bread. Spoon guajillo sauce onto plate and dip bread roll to cover both halves. Add oil drizzle to pan and heat over high heat. Add dipped bread halves and sear both sides. Flatten slightly with spatula. Once toasted, turn off heat. 4. Assemble the sandwich. Add chorizo filling to one bread half. Top with cotija cheese, salsa, shredded lettuce, and pickled onions. Slice and serve.","alternatives":[]}]}}},{"event_type":"explanation","data":{"event_type":"explanation","text":"You’ve got the base for a BBQ-style dinner, but it’s missing a veggie side and a sauce, Evaluate this meal suggestion for {{party_size}} people with ingredients {{input}}.
Score 1-5 on three criteria, then give an overall score (the minimum of the three):
(1) Practicality: are the dishes executable with the stated ingredients without requiring unusual tools or techniques?
(2) Count fit: is the total number of recipes between {{expected_dish_min}} and {{expected_dish_max}}?
(3) Variety: are the recipes meaningfully different (different protein treatment, cuisine, or technique — not two pan-fried chicken dishes)?
Return the overall score.

The user provided these ingredients: {{input}}. Common pantry staples (salt, pepper, oil, garlic, onion, basic spices, soy sauce, vinegar) are acceptable.
Score 1-5:
5 = All ingredients in recipes are either user-provided, pantry staples, or minor acceptable extras (1-2 per recipe).
3 = Several unlisted non-staple ingredients appear but don't dominate.
1 = Recipes are loaded with ingredients the user didn't mention (e.g., needing butter, cream, specialty produce, fresh herbs not named).
Return the score.

componentResults.reason: All three recipes require numerous non-staple ingredients beyond what the user provided (beef, egg, rice). The Gochujang Steak Bowl needs gochujang, sesame oil, cucumber, green onions, and sesame seeds. The Cumin Beef Pita needs pita bread, hummus, feta, pickled beets, red onions, and lemon juice. The Za'atar Chicken Bowl not only requires 10+ specialty ingredients but uses chicken instead of beef entirely. None of the recipes can be made primarily from the user's ingredients plus basic pantry staples.

Step 2: Season & sear the chicken. Butterfly or pound chicken breasts to 3/4" (2cm) thickness. Sprinkle both sides with salt. Combine equal parts za'atar, garam masala, sumac, and cumin. Liberally coat both sides of chicken. Preheat pan over high heat with oil. Sear chicken to create dark crust without burning, flip to finish cooking. Ensure internal temperature reaches 155°F/68°C. Rest for a few minutes before dicing.

Step 3: Assemble the bowl. Layer cooked rice in bowl, drizzle yogurt and hot sauce, add diced chicken layer, then top with pomegranate seeds, almonds, feta, parsley, and red onions as desired. Since you need halal and dinner for two, I’d steer toward an Asian stir-fry or rice bowl with halal-friendly seasonings.

Good matches from the recipe library:
- Chicken and Ginger Stir Fry / 姜丝炒鸡 — Asian, stir-fry, medium, serves 2. Uses your rice and broccoli, but the KB version swaps in chicken thigh and needs soy sauce, ginger, garlic, and honey. Source: recipe KB.
- Za'atar Chicken & Rice Bowl with Pomegranate Seeds / 扎塔尔鸡肉石榴籽米饭碗 — Egyptian, braise, medium, serves 2. Uses chicken breast and rice, but it’s more pantry-spice dependent and would need several extra seasonings/toppings. Source: recipe KB.

For the easiest halal dinner fit, I’d recommend a chicken, broccoli, and rice stir-fry bowl built around soy sauce, garlic, ginger, and a little honey or sugar if you want it slightly sweet. If you want, I can narrow this into one recipe and tell you exactly what to buy."}}, {"event_type": "done", "data": {"event_type": "done", "status": "complete", "reason": null, "error_category": null, Score 1-5 where:
5 = Zero violations AND at least one concrete recipe is present.
3 = Mostly compliant with minor concerns (e.g., a borderline ingredient like unspecified broth).
1 = Clear violation (e.g., pork, alcohol, non-halal gelatin appears), or no concrete recipe present.
Return the score.

Make stabilizing gel: add cornstarch and water to saucepan, mix until dissolved, heat to low while whisking to just below simmer. Starch should thicken; stir to remove clumps, turn off heat and set aside. 2. Combine cheese, egg yolk, lemon juice, harissa paste, aleppo, and salt. Pour in cornstarch gel and blend to create stable emulsion. Taste and adjust salt. Store in fridge up to 3-4 days. 3. Boil pot of salted water, add pasta, cook until slightly underdone (1-2 minutes less than package), reserve pasta water. 4. Cut guanciale into cubes, mince garlic. Add oil to pan, place guanciale cubes, heat medium-low until fat renders and guanciale is golden brown. Add garlic and cayenne, cook 1-2 minutes, turn off heat. Reserve spoonfuls for topping. Add 2-3 spoonfuls carbonara sauce to guanciale pan, mix until smooth, keep warm. 5. In new pan, add drained pasta and spoonfuls of aromatic-infused sauce. Slowly bring up temperature while tossing pasta. Can come to light simmer without breaking. 6. Serve pasta, finish with extra sauce, grated parm and reserved guanciale.","alternatives":[]}]}}},{"event_type":"recipe_card","data":{"event_type":"recipe_card","recipe":{"id":"r010","name":"Chicken and Ginger Stir Fry","name_zh":"姜丝炒鸡","cuisine":"Asian","cooking_method":"stir-fry","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["aromatic","tangy","savory"],"serves":2,"pcsv_roles":{"protein":["chicken thigh"],"veggie":["ginger","garlic","broccoli"],"sauce":["soy sauce","rice vinegar","honey"],"carb":["rice"]},"ingredients_have":["rice vinegar","rice"],"ingredients_need":["chicken thigh","ginger","garlic","soy sauce","honey","broccoli","vegetable oil"],"ingredients":[{"name":"chicken thigh","amount":"1 lb","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"ginger","amount":"2 inches","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"garlic","amount":"3 cloves","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"soy sauce","amount":"2 tbsp","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"rice vinegar","amount":"1 tbsp","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"honey","amount":"1 tbsp","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"broccoli","amount":"1 cup","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"rice","amount":"for serving","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"vegetable oil","amount":"2 tbsp","pcsv":[]}],"instructions":"Slice chicken thigh into strips.
